session.name: MPI-8/MPI-49: 0.0.4 bug batch + version bump + delta builds + Windows/Linux update-flow test

goal.original: Resume MPI-8: fix the three 0.0.3 on-host bugs (models-folder-on-install, Linux start.sh, Pause/Resume button) plus MPI-48, bump 0.0.4, build both deltas, re-test update-from-zip on a fresh 0.0.3 install on Windows + Linux.
goal.summary: 0.0.4 cut (commit 61cd926) with all four fixes; true delta bundles built for all 3 platforms (Windows local, Linux+mac via mpi-ci run 27223953212). Windows + Linux update-from-zip applied successfully onto fresh 0.0.3 installs. BUG B (Linux start.sh 'Run as program') verified on the laptop. A NEW regression — the applier stripped exec bits — was found and fixed (commit d351c41), shipping from 0.0.5. Still unverified on a box: BUG A (models folder), BUG C (no pause button), MPI-48 (gallery icons) — must be checked on Windows (the GPU-less Linux laptop cannot generate). macOS update never tested. Online update path (update.bat/.sh) deferred to 0.0.5 (needs a private-repo release asset).

completed: BUG A (models folder on first-run install) FIXED: chosen root now travels through the /engine/download request body to the post-extract YAML write (routes/engine.js step 6, ~L429-455), surviving the extract scrub that previously wiped the pre-download set-path YAML. UI sends {modelsRoot} in the /engine/download POST (MpiEngineInstall.js). Empty input still resolves to default. NOT yet verified on a box (needs a real fresh engine provision).

BUG B (Linux start.sh not launching via file-manager 'Run as program') FIXED + VERIFIED: setsid --fork double-forks the app to init (escaping Nautilus's tracked process group), returns immediately, no trailing &; fallbacks = backgrounded setsid then nohup. scripts/portable/linux/start.sh. User confirmed 'Run as program' launches the app on the laptop.

BUG C (engine-install Pause/Resume button vanishes mid-download) FIXED by REMOVING the pause/resume UI entirely (user decision: only the engine archive was ever pausable; once done it became a dead button). MpiEngineInstall.js (markup/mounts/handlers/state/visibility all removed; kept _downloadState for UW-deps pulsation) + MpiEngineInstall.css (orphan __button-group rule removed). Backend /engine/pause|resume routes left untouched (dead, harmless; MPI-54 may reuse). Pause/resume remains for model downloads only. NOT yet verified on a box.

MPI-48 (gallery fav/reuse icons overlap as UI scales / in preview) FIXED + DEV-VERIFIED: wrapped both buttons in a single __top-actions flex column (gap-based) instead of two absolutely-positioned wraps with hardcoded rem offsets. Live-measured with playwright (gap constant 6.4px all states, scales to 12.8px at 2x zoom, no overlap). User re-verified via npm start. MPI-48 moved to DONE.

Version bump 0.0.3 -> 0.0.4 (pure patch, commit 61cd926): appVersion.js + package.json (both 0.0.4, agree), releaseNotes.js entry, docs/releases/2026-06-09-v0.0.4.md. Pre-release test skipped (engine-backed, irrelevant for ops-unchanged patch).

Delta bundles built + verified TRUE deltas (0.0.3 -> 0.0.4): Windows local (node scripts/build-portable.mjs --platform win32 --arch x64 --from-manifest release-baselines/windows-x64.json --no-source-manifest) = 266 changed files, buildHash 61cd926037b4, at D:/CubricStudio/Vision/Builds/CubricVision-windows-x64-update-v0.0.4.zip. Linux+mac via mpi-ci run 27223953212 (gh workflow run build-portable.yml --repo MadPonyInteractive/Cubric-Vision --ref master -f ref=master -f version=0.0.4) all green; Linux delta = 19 files; artifacts at D:/CubricStudio/Vision/Builds/mpi-ci-{linux-0.0.4,darwin-arm64-0.0.4}/.
Update-from-zip applied on Windows (drag zip onto update-from-zip.bat; user-verified 0.0.3->0.0.4 valid) and Linux (terminal sh ./update-from-zip.sh <zip>).

APPLIER exec-bit regression FIXED (commit d351c41): apply-update.cjs copied files with fs.copyFileSync (no mode preservation) so updated launchers landed non-executable on Linux/mac after an update -> no 'Run as program', double-click opened as text. restoreExecBit() now reapplies the bundle file's mode + forces +x on .sh/.command/electron binary; Windows no-op; chmod failures non-fatal. Recovered the live 0.0.4 install with manual chmod. SHIPS FROM 0.0.5 (takes effect only in a bundle built with it).

Knowledge preserved + committed: build-experience-log.md new 2026-06-09 section (3 update-flow traps + delta facts); MPI-49 validation.md (0.0.4 cycle status); MPI-48 -> done, MPI-49 -> doing; MPI-48 card description updated with real scope. All pushed (master at ac18f29).
pending: VERIFY on the Windows box (it can generate; the Linux laptop cannot): BUG A (fresh engine install with a custom models folder keeps the chosen root in extra_model_paths.yaml base_path), BUG C (engine install shows NO pause/resume button and runs through), MPI-48 (gallery fav/reuse icons no overlap at zoom/preview post-update).
The user will independently address MPI-51, MPI-54, MPI-57 before the next session; those land in the NEXT bump (0.0.5) alongside the already-committed applier exec-bit fix.

0.0.5 cycle: bump 0.0.4 -> 0.0.5 (mpi-version-bump), REFRESH release-baselines/{linux-x64,windows-x64,darwin-arm64}.json to the 0.0.4 update manifests FIRST so 0.0.5 deltas against 0.0.4 (currently they are the 0.0.3 baselines). Then build deltas (Windows local --from-manifest + mpi-ci dispatch) and re-test.

0.0.5: TEST THE ONLINE UPDATE PATH (update.bat / update.sh) — user wants this exercised, ideally against the PRIVATE repo (MadPonyInteractive). Needs a real/pre-release GitHub Release asset on a private repo; figure out whether update.bat/update.sh can pull from a private repo (auth) or whether a private pre-release tag works. This was deferred all cycle because it needs a release asset.

macOS update-from-zip never tested (no mac build/test this cycle); the applier exec-bit fix covers .command + the Electron.app binary but is unverified on real Apple hardware. See build-experience-log macOS pre-build checklist.

DEFERRED (optional, discussed): improve update-from-zip launchers to auto-detect a CubricVision-*update*.zip next to them + prompt fallback + pause, so Linux/mac get a no-terminal path and Windows stops flashing the usage line on double-click. Apply to all 3 launchers; only helps from the next bundle.

DEFERRED: document the delta flow + --from-manifest + release-baselines refresh-after-release step in the mpi-version-bump skill and docs/releases/portable-distribution-contract.md (carried from prior handoff, still open).

task_card.title: Test portable update flow (Windows + Linux) with a version bump

completed: build-experience-log.md: appended 2026-06-09 'Linux update-flow testing' section (applier exec-bit trap, start.sh Run-as-program vs terminal, arg-required update-from-zip / Windows-only drag-drop, delta-bundle facts, next-tester checklist). Committed ac18f29.
MPI-49 validation.md written with the full 0.0.3->0.0.4 cycle: PASS list, the applier regression+fix, NOT-yet-verified list, UX note. Committed ac18f29.
Board: MPI-48 -> done, MPI-49 -> doing; events appended; MPI-48 card description updated with the real (UI-scale) scope. Committed ac18f29.
Commits this session: 61cd926 (0.0.4 bump + 4 fixes), d351c41 (applier exec-bit fix), ac18f29 (tracking + log). All pushed; origin/master == local at ac18f29.
pending: After 0.0.4 is fully verified on Windows + the 0.0.5 cycle: refresh release-baselines/ to the 0.0.4 manifests, and document the delta/--from-manifest/baseline-refresh flow in mpi-version-bump + portable-distribution-contract.md.
When the online update path is tested (0.0.5), record the private-repo auth mechanism + result in MPI-49 validation.md and build-experience-log.md.

next_action.description: Resume the 0.0.4 update-flow validation. FIRST verify on the WINDOWS box (the GPU-less Linux laptop cannot generate): (A) a fresh engine install with a custom models folder keeps the chosen root in extra_model_paths.yaml base_path; (B/C) the engine install shows NO pause/resume button and runs through; MPI-48 gallery fav/reuse icons do not overlap at zoom/preview post-update. The applier exec-bit fix (commit d351c41) is already committed and ships from 0.0.5 — do NOT rebuild 0.0.4 for it. The user is separately doing MPI-51/MPI-54/MPI-57 for the next bump. When ready for 0.0.5: REFRESH release-baselines/ to the 0.0.4 manifests BEFORE building, bump 0.0.4->0.0.5, build deltas, and TEST THE ONLINE UPDATE PATH (update.bat/update.sh) — ideally against the private MadPonyInteractive repo (needs a release/pre-release asset + private-repo auth investigation).

key_decisions: BUG C resolved by REMOVING engine-install pause/resume UI, not by fixing button visibility — user decided a control that can only pause the engine archive (not the parallel UW model deps) is pointless once the archive finishes. Backend pause routes left intact for MPI-54 reuse.

MPI-48 fixed with a flex container (user's suggestion) over the agent's original magic-number-offset approach — self-sizing, zoom/state-invariant. Verified by live playwright measurement, not just code reasoning.
Applier exec-bit fix rolls into 0.0.5, NOT a 0.0.4 rebuild (user choice) — the live 0.0.4 install is recovered via manual chmod and otherwise valid for testing the other fixes.
0.0.4 = pure patch. release-baselines/ are still the 0.0.3 manifests, so 0.0.4 deltas were against 0.0.3 automatically. They must be refreshed to 0.0.4 BEFORE the 0.0.5 build.
Online update path testing deferred to 0.0.5 against the private repo — needs a release asset; private-repo auth for update.bat/.sh is an open question to investigate.
constraints: Do NOT touch the backend ResumableDownloader pause/abort path (fragile; memory project_ndh_resumable_downloads). BUG C was a pure frontend removal.
Always dispatch mpi-ci with -f ref=master (dispatcher input default ref is 'main' but the branch is 'master').
For TEST builds pass --no-source-manifest so the committed baseline/source manifest is not clobbered. Local Windows builds output to D:/CubricStudio/Vision/Builds.

update-from-zip.{bat,sh,command} are ARG-REQUIRED. Windows: drag the zip onto the .bat (Explorer passes %1) OR run from cmd with the path. Linux/mac: file managers do NOT pass drops to scripts -> must run from a terminal with the path.
Generation-tied checks (BUG A/C, gallery) must run on the WINDOWS box — the GPU-less Linux laptop cannot generate.

The applier reads the TOP-LEVEL resources/cubric/update-manifest.json. Existing installs prior to 0.0.4 had the asar-stall applier; the 0.0.4 fresh installs have the fixed applier; the 0.0.5+ bundles will carry the exec-bit-preserving applier.
Caveman comms mode active for chat (code/commits/security written normal).
files_modified: routes/engine.js (BUG A: /engine/download body -> _runEngineDownload(chosenModelsRoot) -> step 6 honors chosen root; committed 61cd926)
js/components/Compounds/MpiEngineInstall/MpiEngineInstall.js (BUG A body send + BUG C pause UI removal; committed 61cd926)
js/core/appVersion.js, package.json, js/data/releaseNotes.js, docs/releases/2026-06-09-v0.0.4.md (0.0.4 bump; committed 61cd926)
files_to_read_first: docs/releases/build-experience-log.md (2026-06-09 'Linux update-flow testing' section — the 3 traps)
release-baselines/README.md (delta baseline contract; refresh to 0.0.4 before 0.0.5)

resume_prompt: Read .agents/mpi-kanban/state/handoffs/53d3fd29-ab76-41b9-997d-868dfe2dccb1.json and use mpi-continue to continue from where we left off. You are resuming MPI-49 (portable update-flow testing), under the MPI-8 umbrella. Done this session: cut 0.0.4 (commit 61cd926) with four fixes — BUG A (first-run install now keeps the chosen models folder via the /engine/download body -> engine.js step 6), BUG B (Linux start.sh launches via file-manager 'Run as program' using setsid --fork; user-verified), BUG C (removed the engine-install Pause/Resume UI entirely), and MPI-48 (gallery fav/reuse icons no longer overlap — flex __top-actions column, live-verified, moved to done). Built TRUE delta bundles (0.0.3->0.0.4) for all 3 platforms (Windows local 266 files; Linux+mac via mpi-ci run 27223953212; Linux 19 files) at D:/CubricStudio/Vision/Builds. Windows + Linux update-from-zip applied onto fresh 0.0.3 installs successfully. A NEW regression was found and fixed: the applier (apply-update.cjs) stripped exec bits via fs.copyFileSync, so updated Linux/mac launchers landed non-executable (no 'Run as program') — fixed with restoreExecBit() (commit d351c41), which SHIPS FROM 0.0.5 (recover a current install with chmod +x on the launchers). NEXT: verify the still-unchecked 0.0.4 fixes on the WINDOWS box (it can generate; the GPU-less Linux laptop cannot) — BUG A (chosen models folder kept), BUG C (no pause button, runs through), MPI-48 (no icon overlap post-update). The user is separately doing MPI-51, MPI-54, MPI-57 for the next bump. When ready for 0.0.5: FIRST refresh release-baselines/ to the 0.0.4 manifests (so deltas are against 0.0.4), then bump 0.0.4->0.0.5, build deltas, and TEST THE ONLINE UPDATE PATH (update.bat/update.sh) — ideally against the private MadPonyInteractive repo, which needs a release/pre-release asset and a private-repo auth investigation. Constraints: never touch the backend ResumableDownloader; always dispatch mpi-ci with -f ref=master; for test builds pass --no-source-manifest; update-from-zip is arg-required (Windows drag-drop onto the .bat works, Linux/mac need a terminal). master is at ac18f29; origin == local.
